# Code of Conduct

## What this is about

msgviz is an open project. To keep collaboration productive, the
following rules apply to issues, pull requests, discussions and any
other project channel.

## Expected

- Factual, respectful tone.
- Listen to other perspectives before disagreeing.
- Critique code, not people.
- Ask questions instead of making assumptions.
- Help new contributors.

## Not acceptable

- Insults, personal attacks, discrimination.
- Sexualized language or imagery.
- Publishing others' private data.
- Persistent disruption of discussions.

## Scope

These rules apply wherever the project lives: GitHub issues, pull
requests, code-review comments, repository discussions and any future
channels.

## Consequences

For violations, maintainers may:

1. Issue a private warning.
2. Moderate or remove contributions.
3. For repeated or severe violations, revoke project access.

## Reporting

Report problems by email to the maintainers listed in the repository.
Reports are handled confidentially.

## Basis

These rules are inspired by the
[Contributor Covenant 2.1](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/),
deliberately shortened.
